name: Check Cashing
description_short: Check Cashing Philadelphia, Pennsylvania — United Check Cashing offers check cashing, money transfers, money orders, and bill payment services at their ...
description_long:
United Check Cashing is an established check-cashing service provider operating multiple locations, with a significant presence in Philadelphia's South Broad Street area. The company specializes in converting checks into immediate cash, a service particularly valuable for individuals without bank accounts or those seeking faster access to funds than traditional banking institutions provide. Their service model focuses on convenience and accessibility through extended operating hours and a range of financial services beyond basic check cashing.

The Philadelphia location at 1123 South Broad Street offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including check cashing, money transfers, money orders, bill payment processing, pre-paid MasterCard and Visa cards, notary services, transit pass sales, document copying, faxing, and coin counting. The store maintains expansive hours seven days a week, opening at 8:00 AM most days and extending to midnight on Fridays, with Sunday hours from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. This accessibility is complemented by a toll-free customer service line and local phone support.

United Check Cashing distinguishes itself through its willingness to cash checks payable to businesses, a service not all check-cashing providers offer, particularly appealing to small business owners and sole proprietors. The location's extended Friday hours until midnight and convenient South Philadelphia location on a major thoroughfare provide practical advantages for working customers. The bundled service offerings—combining check cashing with money transfers, bill payment, and notary services—create a one-stop financial services destination for underbanked populations.

Check-cashing services typically charge fees that can range from 1-5% of the check amount depending on check type and amount, though specific fee information is not disclosed on the website. As with most check-cashing operations, customers should understand that while the service provides immediate access to funds, it may be more expensive than traditional banking for frequent check deposits. The company's focus on serving populations with limited banking access is legitimate and valuable, though consumers should comparison-shop fees if checking account access is available to them.
pros: Extended hours until midnight on Fridays and 8 AM-10 PM most other days, accommodating working schedules
Will cash checks payable to businesses, a service not universally offered by competitors
Comprehensive service bundle including money transfers, bill payment, notary, transit passes, and coin counting in one location
Multiple contact methods: toll-free 1-800 number plus local Philadelphia phone line
Early opening at 8 AM and Sunday availability (9 AM-5 PM) provides weekend convenience
Established multi-location chain suggesting operational stability and experience
Convenient South Broad Street location in Philadelphia with accessible storefront
cons: Fee structure not disclosed on website; typical check-cashing fees (1-5%) can be substantially higher than bank deposits
Limited to physical location access only; no online check deposit or mobile service mentioned
Pre-paid card offerings (MasterCard/Visa) typically carry monthly fees and limited functionality compared to traditional bank accounts
No information provided about identification requirements, check type restrictions, or maximum cashing limits
Fax and copy services add cost compared to services included with bank accounts
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked Philadelphia residents needing immediate check conversion without bank account
Small business owners and sole proprietors requiring business check cashing services
Shift workers and night-shift employees needing financial services during extended evening hours
Customers needing bundled services like bill payment and money transfers in one location
diagnosis: United Check Cashing is best for unbanked Philadelphia residents and small business owners needing rapid check conversion with extended accessibility hours. Primary caveat: check-cashing fees (not disclosed but typically 1-5% of check amount) make this service more expensive than traditional banking for frequent use, suitable primarily for those without banking alternatives or emergency cash needs.
